options.name: --output
options.description: Output file path, ./zeroql.json by default Default: "./config.zeroql.json".

options.name: --help
options.description: Shows help text.

commands.name: output
commands.description: Reads output from config file. Command as workaround for MSBuild inability to read json.

options.name: --config
options.description: The generation config file. For example, './zeroql.json'. Use `zeroql config init` to bootstrap.

options.name: --help
options.description: Shows help text.

commands.name: generate
commands.description: Generates C# classes from graphql file.

options.name: --config
options.description: The generation config file. For example, './zeroql.json'. Use `zeroql config init` to bootstrap.

options.name: --schema
options.description: The path to get the schema file. For example, './schema.graphql'

options.name: --namespace
options.description: The namespace for generated client

options.name: --client-name
options.description: The client name for the generated client

options.name: --output
options.description: The output file. For example, './Generated/GraphQL.g.cs'

options.name: --visibility
options.description: The visibility within the assembly for the generated client Choices: "Public", "Internal".

options.name: --warnings-to-ignore
options.description: The warnings to ignore when generating the client. Example: --warnings-to-ignore=CS0168,CS0219

options.name: --scalars
options.description: Custom scalars to use when generating the client. Example: --scalars Point=Geometry.Point --scalars Rect=Geometry.Rect

options.name: --netstandard-compatibility
options.description: Enables netstandard compatibility during generation.

options.name: --force
options.description: Ignore checksum check and generate source code Default: "False".

options.name: --help
options.description: Shows help text.

options.name: --assembly
options.description: The assembly that contains the graphql client. For example, './MyApp.dll'

options.name: --class
options.description: The graphql client class name. For example, 'TestGraphQLClient

options.name: --output
options.description: The output folder. For example, './Queries'

options.name: --help
options.description: Shows help text.

commands.name: pull
commands.description: Pulls the schema from a remote server

options.name: --config
options.description: The generation config file. For example, './zeroql.json'. Use `zeroql config init` to bootstrap.

options.name: --url
options.description: The url to pull the schema from. For example, https://server.com/graphql

options.name: --output
options.description: The output file. For example, './schema.graphql' Default: "schema.graphql".

options.name: --token
options.description: Access Token to use when downloading the schema

options.name: --auth
options.description: Auth scheme to use when downloading the schema

options.name: --headers
options.description: Custom headers to use when downloading the schema. Example: --headers key1=value1 --headers key2=value2

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Timeout in seconds for downloading the schema. Default is 30 seconds

options.name: --help
options.description: Shows help text.

options.name: --help
options.description: Shows help text.

options.name: --version
options.description: Shows version information.
